Interpretation: Chest-pain category was the strongest combined signal, showing that symptom pattern is highly informative for the model.
Heart-rate response during exercise helped separate lower-risk and higher-risk patients.

Health Indicator: Number of major vessels colored by fluoroscopy
Interpretation: The vessel-count feature strongly affected model decisions, but coefficient direction depends on the dataset encoding.
The thalassemia category was an important diagnostic indicator for both Logistic Regression and Random Forest.
Exercise-induced angina was one of the clearest exercise-response indicators in the model.
Exercise-related ST depression contributed meaningful information about cardiac stress response.
Sex contributed to the model but should be interpreted carefully because it is demographic context, not a diagnosis by itself.

Health Indicator: Slope of peak exercise ST segment
Interpretation: The slope of the exercise ST segment added signal about exercise ECG behavior.
Age had weaker combined importance than the strongest clinical indicators in this trained model.
Cholesterol appeared in the top ten but was less influential than chest pain, exercise and diagnostic indicators.
